item.title: Keeping my friends entertained
item.description: Edit: thank y'all for the rewards! Send me anything you think will make me laugh/smile
item.dc:creator: SirBananaPants

item.title: This is done to me, I've done it to other people. I needed to hear this, maybe somone else can learn from it too.
item.description: My partner has some comprehension issues, and I have done this a lot. I feel like a monster. Similarly, I DO NOT like crowds. There are few things as akward to me as having a room full of people simultaneously turn and shout "HEY! YOU DECIDED TO SHOW UP!!!" I know to "normal" people it feels welcoming, but people like me, it is hard to not turn an run.
item.dc:creator: dickblack12

item.title: Well well well how the turntables
item.dc:creator: 3PiecesOfBread

item.title: Good lad
item.description: This is Tiernan McCready. In Bogside, UK He saw three males grab an 18 year old girl and try to get her in their van. He reacted instantly and shouted at the males, led the girl to safety and asked his mother to call the police. Source : https://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/news/northern-ireland/he-just-did-what-he-felt-was-right-praise-for-boy-9-who-foiled-attempt-to-abduct-teenager-38003601.html (originally posted by @ALDSS on 2021-04-12 12:26:47)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: And then we can do something more complicated, like true rocket science

item.title: Human Hair for Oil Spills
item.description: Human hair can absorb five times its weight in oil and, though unconventional, is one of the most sustainable and eco-friendly methods for aiding oil spills. Matter of Trust repurposes human hair, animal fur, wool and fleece to create absorption mats that clean up oil spills, accepting donations from salons, groomers and farmers.
item.dc:creator: 60SecondDocs
